Its oft-cited first occurrence is in the late  Vedic Śatapathabrāhmaṇa 3.2.1.24 where it refers to demoniacal beings who speak  indistinctly, like a foreigner or barbarian. A related verbal formation, mlecchad, 3rd  sin
